summary: Turkey's public engagement with post-quantum cryptography runs through two government bodies, neither of which has yet published a national standard, algorithm choice or migration timeline. BTK, the Information and Communication Technologies Authority, has run a programme themed Cybersecurity and Post-Quantum Cryptology promoting the spread of quantum-resistant cryptography in the country. Separately, the Presidency of Defence Industries (SSB) runs the Turkiye Kuantum Platformu, a national quantum-technologies coordination platform that frames quantum communication as complementary layers of QKD and post-quantum cryptography and points to an updated Quantum Technology Roadmap and a 2024 to 2025 results report. Activity to date is coordination and awareness work rather than binding regulation, and no dated post-quantum migration milestone has been set.
govActors:
BTK | Information and Communication Technologies Authority, ran the Cybersecurity and Post-Quantum Cryptology programme promoting quantum-resistant cryptography
SSB (Cumhurbaskanligi Savunma Sanayii Baskanligi) | Presidency of Defence Industries, runs the Turkiye Kuantum Platformu national quantum-technologies coordination platform referencing post-quantum cryptography and the Quantum Technology Roadmap
obligation: No binding PQC obligation; activity is a national authority awareness programme only.
